refnamediv.refpurpose: Returns uniform numbers from a low discrepancy sequence.

listitem.para: a 1-by-1 matrix of floating point integers, the number of calls to the function.
a 1-by-1 matrix of floating point integers, the spatial dimension.
a 1-by-1 matrix of strings, the name of the sequence (default ldseq = "sobol"). The name can be equal to : "halton", "faure", "reversehalton", "sobol", "niederreiter". See below for details.
a 1-by-1 matrix of boolean, set to %f to use potentially favorable parameters (default = %t).
a evalf-by-n matrix of doubles, the uniform random numbers in [0,1]^n.
a 1-by-1 matrix of floating point integers, the actual number of function evaluations. We have evalf==callf if strict is true and evalf >= callf if strict is false.

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: In dimension n, this function generates callf experiments with the low discrepancy sequence ldseq.
Returns the number of suggested function evaluations evalf and the uniform numbers u in [0,1]^n.
Depending on the value of strict, the function uses different values of evalf, skip and leap.

If strict is true (the default), then
the required number of simulations callf is used, i.e. evalf=callf,
skip=0,
leap=0.

If strict is false, then
a potentially favorable number of simulations evalf is used,
a potentially favorable value of skip is used,
a potentially favorable value of leap is used.

In general, using strict=%f may produce a set of points with greater quality. On the other hand, the value of evalf may be much larger than the value of callf, so that, in practice, it may be necessary to use strict=%t.

If strict=%f, the actual behavior depends on the sequence.
Halton: we use the values computed by lowdisc_haltonsuggest.
Faure: we use the values computed by lowdisc_fauresuggest.
Sobol: we use evalf=2^ceil(log2(callf)), skip=0 and leap=0. We do not use lowdisc_sobolsuggest which may compute excessively large number of simulations.
Niederreiter: we use the values computed by lowdisc_niedersuggest.
Reverse-Halton: we use evalf=callf, skip=0 and leap=0.
The sequences which are available are described in depth in the lowdisc_new function.

refsection.title: Examples
